channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: The Documentary Filmmaker Styling Her Peers for the Oscars
item.description: Rachel Beth Anderson knows firsthand that her fellow documentarians can rarely afford to dress for awards shows. So she started a concierge service designed to help them.
item.dc:creator: Lux Alptraum and Gabby Jones
item.media:description: Ms. Anderson and Ms. Blair fit Mr. Borenstein in a cream-colored Yves Saint Laurent suit, which he initially worried might be more appropriate for the director of “a fun vampire movie.”

item.title: Lily Allen Is on Tour. She Brought Along Some Receipts.
item.description: The designer of Ms. Allen’s “West End Girl” tour talks about the scene with the artist pulling cloth printed with receipts that has become the subject of online speculation.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi

item.title: Liza Minnelli: What, Specifically, Makes Her an Icon
item.description: The things that make Liza Liza.
item.dc:creator: Louis Lucero II
item.media:credit: From left, Bert Stern/Conde Nast, via Getty Images; NBCU Photo Bank/NBC Universal, via Getty Images; Fairchild Archive/Penske Media, via Getty Images; Ron Galella/Getty Images

item.title: Meet Nina Park, the Makeup Artist on Many Actresses’ Lips
item.description: The makeup artist Nina Park’s client list, which includes actresses like Emma Stone and Jessie Buckley, has begun to resemble an awards show ballot.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Maiwenn Raoult for The New York Times
item.media:description: As a teenager, Ms. Park was not allowed to wear makeup, so she taught herself how to apply it imperceptibly before leaving the house.

item.title: After a Stroke, How Does ‘ANTM’s’ J. Alexander See the Runway?
item.description: Teaching models how to strut made J. Alexander, or Miss J, a star. After having a stroke, he now uses a wheelchair. Has it shaped how he sees a catwalk?
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Justin J Wee for The New York Times
item.media:description: J. Alexander at Netflix’s office in Manhattan. The streamer recently released a documentary series in which J speaks about his time on “America’s Next Top Model” and about a stroke he is still recovering from.

item.title: At Paris Fashion Week, Some Intrepid Spirits Are Keeping It Weird.
item.description: Amid complaints that Big Fashion has become bland and boring, some intrepid spirits are keeping it weird.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Iredale

item.title: Carolyn Bessette Kennedy’s Wedding Slip Dress Continues to Shape Modern Bridal Fashion
item.description: Thirty years after Ms. Bessette’s wedding to John F. Kennedy Jr., the FX series “Love Story” is renewing interest, again, in her minimalist style.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lyon

item.title: The ‘Mona Lisa’ as You’ve Never Seen Her
item.description: With its ambitious program of designer collaborations, the Louvre is making a new fashion statement.
item.dc:creator: Tina Isaac-Goizé

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘At 35, I Lost My Virginity’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.

item.title: A Planned Parenthood Clinic, in a Pinch, Turns to Botox
item.description: The move is designed to keep the doors of the clinic open, and is a reflection of the increasingly blurred lines between the beauty industry and health care.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta

item.title: Donna Kelce’s Home Renovation Is Taking Over the Internet
item.description: A TMZ headline about the mother of the football players Travis and Jason Kelce has inspired jokes online.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:description: Donna Kelce briefly became a main character of the internet.

item.title: In Japan, Making Wooden Kokeshi Dolls
item.description: A Japanese artisan demonstrates the skill needed to produce kokeshi.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Morelli and Andrew Faulk
item.media:description: The family of Teruyuki Hiraga has been making kokeshi, or traditional Japanese wooden dolls, for eight generations.

item.title: Off the Runway at Paris Fashion Week
item.description: There’s more to fashion week than runway shows. Here, a short list of some high spots.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Iredale

item.title: My Husband’s Sisters Are Suing Him Over Their Father’s Estate. Help!
item.description: A reader is unsure of the path forward with her sisters-in-law after a contentious lawsuit that accused her husband of mishandling their father’s trust.
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes

item.title: Jessie Buckley Became a Mother for ‘Hamnet.’ Then She Became a Real One. (Encore)
item.description: The Oscar-nominated actress talks about the primal love and grief of motherhood.
item.dc:creator: Anna Martin, Reva Goldberg, Emily Lang, Davis Land, Christina Djossa, Amy Pearl, Sara Curtis, Elisa Gutierrez, Jen Poyant, Lynn Levy, Daniel Ramirez and Dan Powell
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times; Background: Brian Rea; Inset photo: Chris Pizzello/Invision, via Associated Press

item.title: ‘Wool Skirts’ Reveals Fashion History Through Thrift-Store Finds
item.description: “Wool Skirts,” an exhibition of one woman’s 40-year thrift-store collection, reveals a rich tapestry of clothing manufacturing and feminist history.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Pilat

item.title: James Fishback, a Republican Candidate for Florida Governor, Is Running on Rage Bait
item.description: Young conservatives in Florida are fascinated by James Fishback, a long-shot gubernatorial candidate known for his provocative online posts.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Taylor Pemberton
item.media:credit: Scott McIntyre for The New York Times
item.media:description: James Fishback during an event at Luv’n Oven Ale House in Sunrise, Fla., in February. Even in person, Mr. Fishback, 31, is channeling a mode of politics that finds its energy through posting online.

item.title: Is a Private-Island Paradise in Turks and Caicos Worth the Price? I Went to Find Out.
item.description: What it’s like to stay on a private island so luxurious that there are no price tags and so quiet that “even the wind feels guilty for making noise.”
item.dc:creator: Danial Adkison

item.title: A Beadwork Artist Pretties Up Horses with Precision and Creativity
item.description: The beadwork artist Beverly Moran has impressed horse and art lovers with her intricate designs and detailed workmanship.
item.dc:creator: Janelle Conaway
item.media:credit: Brad Trone for The New York Times
item.media:description: Beverly Moran, a member of the Standing Rock Sioux Tribe, started teaching herself to bead three decades ago, after she and her daughter began attending powwows.

item.title: Textile Designs That Elevate the Humble Dining Table
item.description: Sarah Espeute, who founded the Marseille brand Oeuvres Sensibles, creates elegant tablecloths that “make people smile.”
item.dc:creator: Heike Blümner
item.media:credit: Jonathan Stokes for The New York Times
item.media:description: A tablecloth made by Oeuvres Sensibles featuring a dinner scene. Most of the company’s products are embroidered by hand.

item.title: Swaine London Embraces Handmade British Tradition
item.description: From leather goods to umbrellas to hats, the London-based company looks to expand its offerings and locations.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Felder
item.media:credit: Jeremie Souteyrat for The New York Times
item.media:description: Umbrellas on display at the entrance of Swaine London’s flagship store on New Bond Street in London.

item.title: Felt Cute, Until They Gave Their Husbands the Phone
item.description: Perfect lighting and backdrops do not guarantee a great photo, as one social media trend highlights. Even professionals are not immune.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

item.title: At Miu Miu’s Paris Fashion Week Show, Miuccia Prada Made Dressing Easy
item.description: It was the motto of the season. Miuccia Prada got it right at Miu Miu, but Nicolas Ghesquière at Louis Vuitton made it hard.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: Louis Vuitton’s luxury nomads, wandering through an alien world.

item.title: Scarf Anxieties
item.description: At Loewe and Celine, scarves as poufy as comforters have our critic wondering why fashion designers can’t leave well enough alone.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:description: Respiratorily risky duvet scarves at Loewe.

item.title: Why Is Everyone Saying the Word Tranche?
item.description: With the release of a certain bunch of documents, a 16th century word is suddenly ubiquitous.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley

item.title: People Are Obsessed With Matthieu Blazy’s Chanel
item.description: To understand the shopping frenzy, look to Matthieu Blazy’s new collection.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: Mania on the Sales Floor at Chanel
item.description: Customers withstood gate-keeping, bomb threats and heartbreakingly low inventory as a new era of Chanel hit stores during Paris Fashion Week.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Iredale
item.media:description: Mania on the sales floor: At Chanel boutiques in Paris, some customers wouldn’t take “non” for an answer.

item.title: Fashionable Wall Street Bros Set Off a Social Media Storm
item.description: They said yes to an Interview magazine fashion shoot. Then came the haters.
item.dc:creator: Guy Trebay
item.media:description: A fierce online reaction greeted a pictorial featuring junior bankers in high-end suits.

item.title: The Charm of TikTok’s ‘Mystery Scoops’
item.description: Shop owners selling boxes of merchandise that correspond to spoonfuls of trinkets are feeding our appetite for nostalgia, A.S.M.R. and dopamine — for $50 a pop.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Roy

item.title: Reviews of Celine and Balenciaga at Paris Fashion Week
item.description: At Celine, Michael Rider calls an end to the oversize. At Balenciaga, Pierpaolo Piccioli gets swamped.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: Loewe, Schiaparelli and Junya Watanabe Have Fun With Fashion
item.description: Loewe was a hoot. Junya Watanabe, a sartorial satire. And wait until I tell you about the Schiaparelli kitten heels.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: Thaddeus Mosley, Sculptor Who Found Fame in His Last Decade, Dies at 99
item.description: A self-taught artist, he turned reclaimed wood into striking abstract works influenced by Brancusi, Noguchi and African art.
item.dc:creator: Will Heinrich
item.media:credit: Ross Mantle for The New York Times
item.media:description: Thaddeus Mosley at his studio in Pittsburgh in 2023. Of the racial and economic barriers he faced, he said: “I knew that I wasn’t going to get the same recognition, have the same opportunity. But I tried, because it’s something I wanted to do.”

item.title: What Clothes Feel New Now?
item.description: In Paris, Loewe and Polo bring some much needed color and uplift.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: This Work Zoom Shouldn’t Have Gone X-Rated
item.description: Plus, how to deal when your pal is the toxic one at the office.
item.dc:creator: Max Read

item.title: A Panorama of Reimagined Designs
item.description: A look at design-world events, products and people.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Rustic Farm Weddings, Now Even More Refined
item.description: Say goodbye to Mason jars and hello to Scandinavian-inspired barns. This return to agrarian aesthetics is as refined as it is rustic.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Sullivan

item.title: For These Design Materials, It’s Goodbye and Good Riddance
item.description: Not all acts of extinction are to be regretted.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Treffinger
item.media:description: PFAS, so-called forever chemicals, were developed in the early 20th century, and are the reason Teflon cookware doesn’t stick, why many pizza boxes resist grease and why clothing can be waterproof.

item.title: The New York Building That Couldn’t Be Budged
item.description: An Upper East Side townhouse engulfed by new construction tells a very New York story.
item.dc:creator: Ian Volner
item.media:credit: Katherine Marks for The New York Times
item.media:description: 1301 Third Avenue is sandwiched between an 18-story luxury condominium and a 32-story tower.

item.title: At Givenchy, Sarah Burton Dresses the Complex Woman
item.description: Or Sarah Burton, the brand’s creative director, did, in all her multiple incarnations. Rick Owens took Marlene Dietrich punk.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: Stella McCartney Is Awarded the French Medal of Honor
item.description: Stella McCartney commands the axis of cultural power for the French Legion of Honor.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Iredale

item.title: At Some Weddings, Being a Bridesmaid Comes with Strict Rules
item.description: In a TikTok video, an influencer said that she would prefer for bridesmaids not to wear a specific hairstyle to her wedding, igniting a conversation about the strict rules bridesmaids often follow.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:description: A TikTok debate erupted after a bride said she was banning the slicked-back bun from her bridesmaids’ hairstyles.

item.title: Paul Simon, Whoopi Goldberg and Bill Murray Gather for a Night of Rock
item.description: The Love Rocks charity concert has become one of New York’s most decorated nights of rock ’n’ roll.
item.dc:creator: Alex Vadukul and Nina Westervelt

item.title: From 2004: Estée Lauder, Pursuer of Beauty and Cosmetics Titan, Dies at 97
item.description: Her company, known for its creams, powders, ointments and perfumes, was estimated to be worth about $5 billion when it went public in 1995.
item.dc:creator: Richard Severo
item.media:description: Estée Lauder at her office in 1985. She convinced generations of women determined to maintain a youthful appearance that her beauty creams were “jars of hope.”

item.title: From 2019: Agnès Varda, Influential French New Wave Filmmaker, Dies at 90
item.description: She was closely associated with the film movement known as the New Wave, although her reimagining of cinematic conventions predated it.
item.dc:creator: John Anderson

item.title: Tom Junod Would Like to Tell You About His Father
item.description: A star writer from the heyday of magazines reveals the family secret behind his award-winning stories.
item.dc:creator: Steven Kurutz
item.media:credit: Mark Sommerfeld for The New York Times

item.title: The Allure of Traveling to the French Riviera in the Winter
item.description: A region famous for its sun-drenched climate becomes a refreshing retreat when the summer heat, megayachts and swarms of tourists are gone.
item.dc:creator: Alexis Steinman and Gianni Cipriano

item.title: Reclaimed Trees Get Creative Reuse With New York Heartwoods
item.description: Megan Offner finds creative and comforting ways to salvage doomed trees.
item.dc:creator: Jane Margolies
item.media:description: Megan Offner is the founder of New York Heartwoods. The circular wood products consultancy reclaims trees, providing a more meaningful and sustainable alternative to commercial-grade lumber.

item.title: From Endangered Plants Come Whiffs of Inspiration
item.description: How scientists, conservationists and the fragrance industry are working to revive extinct scents and perpetuate threatened ones.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: At Alaïa, Saying Goodbye With the Perfect Coat
item.description: Assessing Pieter Mulier’s final Alaïa show — and his tenure.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: Where real luxury is in the fit.

item.title: Thank God the Lights Went Out
item.description: If he couldn’t see me or my place, maybe he wouldn’t judge me either.
item.dc:creator: William J. O’Brien

item.title: Brooke Hyland of ‘Dance Moms' Marries Brian Thalman
item.description: Brian Thalman became familiar with Brooke Hyland while watching her on “Dance Moms.” The two met seven years after her final season on the reality TV show.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

item.title: They Met in a Chemistry Class, but the Connection Came Later
item.description: Jordan Goldberg and Adam Rossman were friends at the University of Texas at Austin. Years later, after building a life in New York, they married in Austin.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: On the Ninth Hole, Her Dad Took a Swing at Playing Cupid
item.description: Taylor Ross’s father asked Noah Koeppel, his new golf buddy’s son, if he wanted to meet his daughter. He was game. She wasn’t.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: Their Favorite Projects? Inventing Cocktails and Card Games.
item.description: For Jai Lennard, a photographer, and Alelli Tanghal, an art director, collaboration has long been the foundation of their romance.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova

item.title: Tom Ford Thrills
item.description: Haider Ackermann serves up what is likely the show of the season.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: At Tom Ford, Haider Ackermann Dresses Adult Desire
item.description: At Tom Ford, Haider Ackermann explores what might happen just before suits come undone.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: The Boys in Trueblood Came to Rock
item.description: The four teenage brothers mostly play the oldies. (To them, the oldies are Coldplay and Maroon 5.)
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann and Elinor Kry

item.title: The Villa Beer in Vienna Opens as a Museum and Cultural center
item.description: The newly renovated Villa Beer in Vienna is a modernist masterpiece with a dark past.
item.dc:creator: Eve M. Kahn
item.media:credit: Andreas Meichsner for The New York Times
item.media:description: After decades of moldering, the Villa Beer property opens as a museum and cultural center, with some of time’s ravages on view.
